_doc: Physics FEEL tuning — the small set of knobs a live playtest tweaks to make the restaurant feel rigid/solid instead of bouncy. Read by shared/physics.js (via config.js) and applied identically to BOTH the host's authoritative world and every client's prediction world (one source, so tuning can never desync). Feel cannot be verified headless — these are the dials to turn between playtests. See memory/notes/physics.md (feel-tuning section).

_notes.groundStickSpeed: Pass #5 anti-bob. Downward stick velocity (m/s) a grounded, non-jumping player keeps instead of vy=0, so the swept controller holds ground contact and computedGrounded() stays stable (vy=0 made it flip every other substep on dynamic props = the standing-on-objects bob + flaky jump). Must satisfy value/60 >= controllerOffset (0.02), i.e. >= 1.2; the controller stops the press at the surface so there is no visible sinking.
_notes.depenetrate: Bug 2 anti-tunnel failsafe. When true, a player capsule that STARTS a physics substep genuinely inside solid geometry (e.g. a wall-top jump that landed it a hair inside a thin edge) is snapped back to its last collision-free position instead of being allowed to fall through the wall. Uses a skin-shrunk capsule test so ordinary resting-on-a-surface contact never trips it. Set false only if a live playtest shows it stuttering — it can only add stability.
_notes.restitution: 0 = nothing bounces. A restaurant has no trampolines. Applied to every collider (dynamic props, static fixtures, floor, walls, ground). Rapier's default is already 0, so this is belt-and-suspenders + a single dial if a future map ever wants bounce.
_notes.numSolverIterations: Contact-solver iterations (Rapier default 4). Raised to make stiff contacts resolve fully in one step — the main fix for both sinking-into-props penetration and standing-on-object bobbing. 12-16 is the useful range; higher costs CPU every frame. If a PHONE HOST dips below 60fps, THIS is the first dial to back down (try 8).
_notes.numAdditionalFrictionIterations: Extra friction-solver iterations (Rapier default 4). Kept at 4; more helps props not slide/creep on tables.
_notes.propLinearDamping: Dynamic-prop linear damping (0.3-0.5). Higher = a nudged prop coasts less and settles sooner. Player capsule is kinematic and unaffected.
_notes.propAngularDamping: Dynamic-prop angular damping (~0.4). Higher = less spinning/wobble after a shove. Was 0.7; lowered so a real shove still tumbles a little (the prop-vs-disguise tell) but stops oscillating.
_notes.capGroundedImpulse: Anti-bobbing for standing ON a dynamic prop. When true, a player who is grounded AND standing still stops pushing impulses into the prop underfoot (which the prop would spring back from next frame = the bob). Walking INTO a prop still shoves it (impulses stay on while moving), so the tell is preserved. Kinematic capsule is otherwise untouched.
